description: Fleet-wide distribution of tenant-configured alerting thresholds (#655). Consumes the already-exported user_threshold gauge to give the platform a cross-tenant governance view: for a given (metric, severity), see how every tenant's threshold compares, and surface statistical outliers (Tukey 1.5×IQR fences) that are likely too strict (alert fatigue) or too loose (under-protected). NOTE: a tenant that DISABLED a threshold emits no user_threshold series and is therefore invisible here — disabled = blind spot, not 'in-range'.

panels.title: Tenants configuring this threshold
panels.description: Distinct tenants with a value set (custom or default) for this metric+severity. Disabled thresholds emit no series and are NOT counted. COLOR = sample adequacy for the outlier panels below: red < 4, yellow 4-7, green >= 8. At low counts the Tukey outlier detection is unreliable (see those panels).

targets.expr: count(count by(tenant) (user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"})) or vector(0)

panels.title: Fleet median (P50)
panels.description: Median threshold value across all tenants for the selected metric+severity. The robust 'consensus' value — the recommender (#656) anchors here.

panels.title: P95
panels.description: 95th-percentile threshold value across tenants. Tenants at/above this are in the loosest 5% (under-protection risk if the rule compares with '>').

panels.title: IQR (P75 − P25)
panels.description: Interquartile range — the robust spread of the fleet. Wide IQR = tenants disagree a lot on this threshold; near-zero = everyone clustered (usually on the default).

targets.expr: quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"})

panels.title: Upper / lower Tukey fence
panels.description: Statistical outlier boundaries: P75+1.5·IQR (upper) and P25−1.5·IQR (lower). Tenants outside this band are flagged as outliers in the table below. Unlike a fixed P95 cut, this flags genuine outliers only — it can be empty when the fleet is healthy.

targets.expr: quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) + 1.5 * (quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}))

quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - 1.5 * (quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}))

panels.title: Outliers
panels.description: Tenants beyond the 1.5×IQR fence (either side). ⚠️ Unreliable when the fleet is default-dominated (IQR→0 flags every customizer) or small (red/yellow Tenants panel) — read with the 'Δ from median' table + histogram, not alone. 0 here does NOT guarantee a clean fleet at low N.

targets.expr: (count((user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"} > scalar(quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) + 1.5 * (quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"})))) or (user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"} < scalar(quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - 1.5 * (quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"})))))) or vector(0)

panels.title: Threshold value distribution across tenants
panels.description: Histogram of the current threshold value held by each tenant for the selected metric+severity. Reveals shape the average hides: a bimodal spread (two clusters) or a long tail of permissive outliers. The tall bar is usually the platform default.

panels.title: Fleet quantile band over time (P5 / P50 / P95)
panels.description: How the fleet's threshold distribution drifts over time. Widening band = growing disagreement / config drift across tenants; a P50 that creeps up over weeks is the 'threshold rot' signal #656 automates (e.g. a tenant loosened during an incident and never tightened back).

panels.title: All tenants — value & deviation from median
panels.description: Every tenant's current threshold for this metric+severity, with its signed deviation from the fleet median. THIS IS THE ROBUST PRIMARY VIEW — deviation-from-median stays meaningful even when most tenants sit on the default (where the Tukey outlier table on the right degenerates). Sort by Δ to triage who sits furthest from consensus, by magnitude.

targets.expr: user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"} - scalar(quantile(0.5, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}))

panels.title: ⚠️ Statistical outliers (Tukey 1.5×IQR)
panels.description: Tenants beyond P75+1.5·IQR (side=high) or below P25−1.5·IQR (side=low). High = more permissive (under-protection if the rule is '>'); low = stricter (fatigue risk if '>'); direction is rule-dependent, NOT hardcoded — confirm against the rule pack. ⚠️ RELIABILITY — degenerates in two common regimes: (1) when most tenants are on the default, IQR→0 and this lists EVERY customizer (triage by magnitude via the 'Δ from median' table on the left); (2) at low tenant counts (red/yellow Tenants panel) it can both miss real extremes and over-flag trivial deviations. Treat as a statistical hint, not ground truth.

targets.expr: label_replace(user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"} > scalar(quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) + 1.5 * (quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}))), "side", "high", "tenant", ".*") or label_replace(user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"} < scalar(quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - 1.5 * (quantile(0.75, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}) - quantile(0.25, user_threshold{metric="$metric", severity="$severity", component=~"$component"}))), "side", "low", "tenant", ".*")

list.name: metric
list.label: Metric
list.description: Threshold key (e.g. mysql_connections, container_cpu). One metric at a time keeps the value distribution scale-coherent.

list.name: severity
list.label: Severity
list.description: warning and critical thresholds differ in scale — compare one at a time.

list.name: component
list.label: Component
list.description: Optional drill-down (mariadb, redis, ...). 'All' is safe when the metric key implies a single component.

title: Dynamic Alerting — Fleet Threshold Distribution
